newsSinceJun6: Stock extended a sharp pullback (-4.35% Jun 9 to $149.59, ~8-9% over the week) driven by a sector-wide reassessment of AI-networking names after softer AI guidance from a peer, plus insider sales (Bechtolsheim sold 240K shares Jun 4). Fundamentals moved the other way: Arista raised its AI Ethernet revenue outlook (AI revenue expected to more than double in 2026 across 100+ AI customers) and launched the 7060XE7 1.6T rack-scale AI platform with Meta/Microsoft support. Move judged flow/sentiment-driven with thesis intact; YTD has compressed to ~12% from 30%+.

sources.label: Simply Wall St - Arista raises AI Ethernet revenue outlook
TradingKey - ANET June 5 decline on AI-networking sector reassessment

verdicts.rankRationale: The week's flow-driven sell-off with an intact (even improving) AI-networking thesis adds a little asymmetry, but ANET remains a $187B widely-owned benchmark name that sits far from this page's passive/power bottleneck core, so rank 93 monitor status still fits.
verdicts.bull: Raised AI Ethernet outlook and the 7060XE7 1.6T launch confirm Arista is the scale-out networking standard; a ~9% weekly drawdown on sector sentiment resets entry without any thesis damage.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI exposure but not a hidden passives/power bottleneck; mega-cap scale and broad ownership cap residual alpha on this list regardless of the dip.
verdicts.bear: Peer's soft AI guidance could be an early read-across on AI networking order timing, and heavy insider selling (Bechtolsheim 240K shares) adds a sentiment overhang.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI Ethernet revenue more than doubling in 2026 and 1.6T platform ramp with Meta/Microsoft.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Sector-wide AI capex digestion plus hyperscaler customer concentration turning the sentiment drawdown into a fundamental one.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down call was premised on a 30%+ YTD rerating; that premise has been unwound (YTD now ~12% after a ~9% weekly flow-driven sell-off) while the optics/scale-out thesis strengthened via a raised AI Ethernet outlook and the 1.6T 7060XE7 launch, improving asymmetry on this networking-bucket page.
verdicts.bull: 1.6T platform with Meta/Microsoft support plus AI revenue guided to more than double in 2026 directly validate the 800G/1.6T scale-out bandwidth thesis at a now-cheaper price.
verdicts.neutral: Execution and pricing in AI-cluster Ethernet remain the watch items; the pullback restores some upside but valuation is still rich versus fair-value estimates (~$137-188 range across services).
verdicts.bear: If the peer's soft AI guidance signals a broader AI networking order air-pocket, Arista's hyperscaler-concentrated revenue would be hit hardest, and insider sales suggest those closest see limited near-term upside.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 7060XE7 1.6T ramp and AI Ethernet revenue doubling through 2026 deployments at 100+ AI customers.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI networking demand reassessment spreading from peers to Arista's own order book, with white-box/Nvidia Ethernet competition pressuring pricing.
